texto: No matter where I went, where I’ve gone, what I’ve seen and what I’ve done…you have never not been wit’ me. You’ve always been my home.
explicacao: Citação de M Robinson sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “No matter where I went, where I’ve gone, what I’ve seen and what I’ve done…you have never not bee…” articula amor, alpha-male, angst em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como luto e termino.

frase_original: No matter where I went, where I’ve gone, what I’ve seen and what I’ve done…you have never not been wit’ me. You’ve always been my home.

texto: When I saw him at the cemetry. I didn't even realize I was walking toward him until I was standing in front of him. It was like beging pulled by a string he was controlling. Luring me right to him. Then when we spoke, I could see it in his eyes, hear it in his voice... he loves me. And not in the 'I love you, I love you,too' kind of way. It's in the 'You're are my person, my lobster, my everything,' and that alone tells me that what we had was true.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a M. Robinson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “When I saw him at the cemetry. I didn't even realize I was walking toward him until I was standin…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, love-quotes, cemetry pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: When I saw him at the cemetry. I didn't even realize I was walking toward him until I was standing in front of him. It was like beging pulled by a string he was controlling. Luring me right to him. Then when we spoke, I could see it in his eyes, hear it in his voice... he loves me. And not in the 'I love you, I love you,too' kind of way. It's in the 'You're are my person, my lobster, my everything,' and that alone tells me that what we had was true.
